parameters.content:
### When does this fire?
In our setup we have a general team in Linear where we post new tickets to. Additionally, the bug needs to have a certain label and the description needs to be filled. 
You're of course free to adjust this to your needs
👇

nodes.name: Set me up

assignments.name: teams
assignments.value:
- [Adore][Is responsible for every persona that is not Enterprise. This includes signup journeys, trials, n8n Cloud, the Canvas building experience and more, the nodes detail view (NDV), the nodes panel, the workflows list and the executions view] 
- [Payday][Is responsible for the Enterprise persona. This includes making sure n8n is performant, the enterprise features SSO, LDAP, SAML, Log streaming, environments, queue mode, version control, external storage. Additionally the team looks out for the execution logic in n8n and how branching works] 
- [Nodes][This team is responsible for everything that is related to a specific node in n8n] 
- [Other][This is a placeholder if you don't know to which team something belongs]

nodes.notes: This set node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## Setup
1. Add your Linear and OpenAi credentials
2. Change the team in the `Linear Trigger` to match your needs
3. Customize your teams and their areas of responsibility in the `Set me up` node. Please use the format `[Teamname][Description/Areas of responsibility]`. Also make sure that the teamnames match the names in Linear exactly.
4. Change the Slack channel in the `Set me up` node to your Slack channel of choice.

values.content: I need you to classify a bug ticket and tell me which team should work on it
All possible teams will be described in the following format: [Teamname][Areas of responsibility]

=The possible teams are the following:
 {{ $('Set me up').first().json.teams }}
Which team should work on this bug?
Do not respond with anything else than the name of the team from the list you were given

data.title: cannot scroll the canvas after duplicating or pausing a note
data.description:
## Description

After using the canvas for a while I always had issues where the scrolling would stop working. I finally found a way to reproduce the issue reliably.

## Expected

I would like to always be able to scroll the canvas using CMD + click

## Actual

Sometimes when using the app the scrolling stops working and you have to refresh to get it back to work.

## Steps or workflow to reproduce (with screenshots/recordings)

**n8n version:** \[Deployment type\] \[version\]

1. Add any nodes to the canvas
2. Click either the Duplicate or Pause buttons that appear when hovering over a node
3. Try scrolling using CMD/CTRL + Click. Scrolling should no longer work while it should still work

Created by Omar

parameters.content:
### When does this fire?
In our setup we have a general team in Linear where we post new tickets to. Additionally, the bug needs to have a certain label and the description needs to be filled. 
You're of course free to adjust this to your needs
👇

## Setup
1. Add your Linear and OpenAi credentials
2. Change the team in the `Linear Trigger` to match your needs
3. Customize your teams and their areas of responsibility in the `Set me up` node. Please use the format `[Teamname][Description/Areas of responsibility]`. Also make sure that the teamnames match the names in Linear exactly.
4. Change the Slack channel in the `Set me up` node to your Slack channel of choice.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: linear  O Auth2  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Linear Trigger".
Open the "Only tickets that need to be classified" node (n8n-nodes-base.filter) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Update team" node (n8n-nodes-base.linear) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get all linear teams" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Check if AI was able to find a team" node (n8n-nodes-base.if) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Notify in Slack" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Merge data" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set team ID".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set me up".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
